tiles.text: You can't take a picture of this. It's already gone.

groups.name: Six Feet Under universe
groups.explanation: Nate Fisher (character), The Fisher House (location/setting — the family funeral home), Peter Krause (actor who played Nate Fisher), and 'You can't take a picture of this. It's already gone.' (quote from the finale) are all from Six Feet Under.

groups.name: Crime dramas whose final season was set several years after the previous one via a time jump
groups.explanation: The Killing (Season 4 jumped ahead), Top of the Lake (Series 2 'China Girl' jumped years forward), Happy Valley (Series 3 jumped several years into the future), and Broadchurch (Series 3 introduced a new case years later) all used significant time jumps across their runs to begin a new chapter — each show returned after a multi-year gap with a story set years after the prior season.

groups.name: TV characters who were introduced as comic relief but were given a devastating tragedy storyline in a later season
groups.explanation: Andy Sipowicz (NYPD Blue) was initially gruff comic relief before losing his son and wife in gut-wrenching arcs; Gil Grissom (CSI) started as a quirky entomologist before a hearing-loss and isolation storyline; Vic Mackey (The Shield) began with dark comedy before his entire family was destroyed as consequence; Michael Chiklis played The Thing in films but on TV (The Shield) his Vic Mackey was the trap — this group's real link is these characters all started with comedic or quirky introductions but were given a storyline where they lost a child or immediate family member mid-run.

groups.name: Fictional small towns that contain a color hidden inside their name
groups.explanation: Each fictional TV town conceals a color word inside it: 'Rust Belt' (Rust — a shade of reddish-brown/orange, from Justified spin-off references); 'Bluebell' (BLUE — the town in Hart of Dixie); 'Aberystwyth' contains no color — CORRECTION replaced: Wayward Pines contains 'PINE' (a shade of green); Bluebell = BLUE; Rust Belt = RUST (reddish color); Aberystwyth — swap: 'Whitechapel' (WHITE). All four fictional locations from TV shows hide a color word: Rust Belt (RUST), Bluebell (BLUE), Wayward Pines (PINE/green family), and Whitechapel (WHITE) — each a small town setting in a TV drama with a hidden color in its name.
